Title: Run first end-to-end game test (GatherWood in Minecraft)
Description:
Validate that the full agent pipeline works against a real Minecraft server.

Pre-conditions:
1. Minecraft server running (vanilla or Paper, 1.16.4+ for pathfinder)
2. npm install run in MineflayerAdapter/
3. Agent:Enabled=true, Agent:Minecraft:* configured in appsettings.json

Test steps:
1. dotnet run --project WebUI.Blazor
2. Watch logs: should see 'Mineflayer adapter starting', 'Bot spawned', 'World adapter connected'
3. POST http://localhost:5000/api/agent/plan {goalName:GatherWood, parameters:{count:5}}
4. Watch bot navigate to a tree and mine 5 logs in-game
5. GET /api/agent/status -- verify Health > 0 and Goal = GatherWood

Success criteria:
- Bot spawns and C# receives spawn event
- MoveToTool dispatches a move command and bot navigates
- MineBlock command executes and blockMined events are received
- GatherWoodGoal.IsComplete returns true when oak_log count >= 5

Known gap: MoveToTool currently sends hardcoded {x:0, y:64, z:0} coordinates. For real wood gathering, Phase 4 adaptive execution (SearchMemory result -> MoveTo coords) must be implemented first.

Comments.Author: AI Agent
Comments.Body: NOTE: MoveToTool sends coordinates from ActionData.Arguments. HtnTaskLibrary.GatherWoodDecompose does not set x/y/z in the MineBlock action -- it relies on the bot being near trees already. For a real test, either manually navigate the bot near trees first, or implement SearchMemory -> MoveTo context injection (Phase 4 adaptive execution) before this test.
